stub: He is a Conservative MP who comes from a white middle class background.
constituency: He is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: He rarely speaks out and votes against his party leadership.
substantive: He opposes the building of the new HS2 railway between major UK cities.
justification: In political debates, he regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: He is a Conservative MP who comes from a white middle class background. He is from a constituency that is not your constituency. He rarely speaks out and votes against his party leadership. He opposes the building of the new HS2 railway between major UK cities. In political debates, he regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
